source: Rocky Linux 'About systemd' guide - [Install] & related commands

questions.q: What does 'WantedBy=multi-user.target' in the [Install] section achieve when the unit is enabled?
options: It links the unit into multi-user.target's wants, so enabling it starts it with that target
questions.why: On enable, a symlink appears in /etc/systemd/system/multi-user.target.wants/, so the unit starts with that target.

questions.q: What does the Alias= directive provide?
options: Additional names for the unit (of the same type/suffix)
questions.why: Alias= gives extra names (same suffix) by which the unit can be referred to once enabled.

questions.q: Which command queries or changes the system time and date?
questions.why: timedatectl manages time/date; hostnamectl the hostname, localectl locale/keyboard, loginctl login sessions.

questions.q: Which command profiles boot performance and shows/checks unit dependencies?
questions.why: systemd-analyze profiles systemd (e.g. boot times) and checks unit files/dependencies.

questions.q: Which command manages the sessions of logged-in users?
